The '''''Deathraven''''' was the personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] named [[Erron Kell]], who constructed the ship sometime after the formation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] from two [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] [[B-wing/E starfighter|B-wing/E2 starfighters]] with the help of [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] at [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. The thirty-two [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long vessel combined the two [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing]] [[Hull/Legends|hulls]], which were obtained from Kell's contacts in the New Republic, around an enlarged central [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. Due to the addition of two [[Ion cannon/Legends|light ion cannons]], the ''Deathraven'' was more heavily armed than the two B-wing/E2s combined, and it boasted a Class 1.0 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] and [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]], and many other improved systems. Kell flew the ship while working as a pilot-for-hire during the reign of the New Republic and the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]], although Kell still preferred to transport the ''Deathraven'' as cargo during long trips due to the cramped nature of the vessel.

==Description==
A unique design built from two [[B-wing/E starfighter|B-wing/E2 starfighters]], the ''Deathraven'' boasted a larger, central [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] with the two [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing]] [[Hull/Legends|hulls]] rotating around it. Due to its thirty-two [[Meter/Legends|meter]] length, the [[Ugly/Legends|modified starship]] was too big to be considered a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], although its cockpit was still uncomfortable for extended travel. However, the ''Deathraven'' did possess two [[Standard month/Legends|months]]' worth of [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] and [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] in case a long trip was necessary. The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] design had a Class 1.0 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], improved [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] and [[Atmospheric thruster|atmospheric]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] performance, improved [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating, and stronger [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], which was due to upgraded [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]]. Instead of requiring a two-person crew like the original B-wing/E2, the ''Deathraven'' only required a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and had room for one passenger along with an increased cargo space of 250 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] in its enlarged cockpit area.<ref name="SOTG" />

In addition to possessing the armaments of the two B-wing/E2 starfighters, the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] was upgraded with two additional [[Ion cannon/Legends|light ion cannons]] to supplement the four original [[assault laser cannon]]s, six light ion [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]], and four [[Proton torpedo launcher/Legends|proton torpedo launchers]]. All of the assault [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] were linked to fire together, as were the [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launchers and light ion cannons. Due to the expanded magazines possessed by the B-wing/E2, the ''Deathraven'' could hold up to forty proton torpedoes when fully loaded. Overall, the design was more powerful than most starfighters, while its speed was more than enough to escape from larger vessels.<ref name="SOTG" />

==History==
The design of the ''Deathravan'' was conceived by [[Erron Kell]], a former [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] pilot who resigned his commission and became a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] due to his dislike of the increased organization of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Through his New Republic contacts, Kell was able to obtain two B-wing/E2s that the New Republic believed were too expensive to repair. While [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] manufactured the original fighters, Kell constructed the new design with help from [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] working for the shipbuilding company known as [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. After all of the modifications and repairs were complete, the ''Deathraven'' was worth around 609,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], which was considerably more than the total 500,000 credits that two B-wing/E2s cost.<ref name="SOTG" />

After the construction of the vessel was complete, Kell used the ''Deathraven'' as his personal starship while working as a mercenary pilot during the reign of the New Republic and the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. During Kell's career, the starship proved to be powerful enough to defeat most small ships, and fast enough to outrun larger ones. In one instance, the ''Deathraven'' destroyed a smaller vessel, which exploded near a [[Star/Legends|stellar]] object. When traveling over long distances, Kell usually shipped the ''Deathraven'' as cargo to avoid being stuck in the small ship for long periods.<ref name="SOTG" />

==Owners and operators==
Although the original B-wing/E2 starfighters used in building the ''Deathraven'' belonged to the New Republic, Erron Kell was able to obtain them through his contacts with the New Republic government. After the ship's construction, Kell used his excellent piloting skills to fly the ship during his career as a mercenary pilot.<ref name="SOTG" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Plus, I just couldn't resist abusing my power just a little. I've had an idea for a particular modified ship ever since seeing Return of the Jedi, so I slipped it into the book.|Owen K.C. Stephens on the ''Deathraven''|<ref name="Stephens">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20011213b_owen&c=rpg|text="Uncle Owen" and His Wonderful Flying Machines}}</ref>}}
[[File:Deathraven-WOTC.jpg|thumb|left|130px|The duplicate ships]]
The ''Deathraven'' was first featured in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[2001]] edition of ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'', which was written by [[Owen K.C. Stephens]]. In the book, the ''Deathraven'' was pictured as destroying a small target in an image<ref name="SOTG" /> created by [[John Gallagher]].<ref name="Gallery">''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]''</ref> However, while the sourcebook referred to the ship's design as unique,<ref name="SOTG" /> a slightly different image in Wizards of the Coast's online ''Starships'' Art Gallery has the ''Deathraven'' leading a pair of ships that share its double-hulled design.<ref name="Gallery" /> This article was written with the assumption that the sourcebook is correct.

Shortly after the release of ''Starships of the Galaxy'', Stephens spoke about the origins of the ''Deathraven'' in an interview that was posted on [[Wizards.com]]. He explained that the design for the ship was an idea he had possessed since he saw [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], and that he used the book as an opportunity to make the ship official. Stephens also stated in the interview that he could not have been more pleased with the artwork provided for the ship.<ref name="Stephens" />
